title: Data Status Report
text: Data Status Report provides overview quality, completeness, readiness camera-trap dataset generating final Ecological Report. helps users check whether main input files, metadata fields, deployment information, observation records, taxonomy, timestamps, annotation information, spatial data complete internally consistent. report especially useful first quality-control step. allows users identify missing values, duplicated records, invalid timestamps, incomplete metadata, spatial issues, inconsistencies deployments observations. Based checks, users can decide whether dataset ready reporting whether correction needed ecological analyses performed. Figure 1. Workflow generating Data Status Report camera-trap data using camtrapReport. Figure 1 summarises camtrapReport checks readiness camera-trap dataset ecological reporting. data loaded camData(), status() function runs automated checks main input files, metadata, deployments, observations, timestamps, taxonomy, spatial information, annotation records. output helps users identify potential issues decide whether dataset ready generating final Ecological Report. view = TRUE, generated report opened browser. provides quick, human-readable overview dataset quality, completeness, consistency.
code: library(camtrapReport)  # Load camera-trap data cm <- camData("cameratrap.zip")  # Generate and view the Data Status Report status(cm, view = TRUE)

title: Data Status Report
text: example Data Status Reports generated camtrapReport different camera-trap monitoring projects. examples show report can used assess dataset completeness, detect possible issues, evaluate whether data ready ecological reporting. Click image open full HTML report.

title: Data Status Report
text: results data-status checks also stored inside camReport object. Users can explore results directly R data_status slot. useful users want inspect underlying tables, extract summaries, use quality-control outputs another workflow. example, species table can inspected check species detected many captures recorded species. Together, status(cm, view = TRUE) cm$data_status provide human-readable HTML report direct access underlying data-quality summaries R. makes Data Status Report useful documenting data readiness, identifying issues, improving dataset generating final Ecological Report.
code: # Explore all data-status outputs stored in the camReport object cm$data_status  # Show the available data-status components names(cm$data_status)  cm$data_status$Spatial cm$data_status$Temporal cm$data_status$Essentials cm$data_status$Annotation cm$data_status$Validation cm$data_status$Species cm$data_status$Visuals # View species summary table cm$data_status$Species$Table

title: Ecological Report
text: Ecological Report main interpretive output camtrapReport. brings processed camera-trap data together clear, scientific-style report narrative text, tables, figures, maps. report summarises key information study context, sampling effort, species detections, activity patterns, spatial patterns, ecological trends. provides reproducible modular workflow automates much reporting process, helping researchers, conservation practitioners, camera-trap users analyse, interpret, communicate camera-trap data efficiently conservation planning decision-making. Figure 1. Workflow generating Ecological Report camera-trap data using camtrapReport. Figure 1 summarises main workflow generating Ecological Report camtrapReport. Camera-trap data first loaded camData(), creating object supports data preprocessing, manipulation, analysis, summarisation, visualisation. processed object passed report() function, generates complete HTML Ecological Report.
code: library(camtrapReport)  # Load camera-trap data cm <- camData("cameratrap.zip")  # Generate the Ecological Report report(cm, view = TRUE)

title: Ecological Report
text: example Ecological Reports generated camtrapReport different camera-trap monitoring projects. Click image open full HTML report.

title: Ecological Report
text: camera-trap data loaded camData(), resulting camReport object can customised generating final Ecological Report. Users can adjust report metadata, select focal species group, define monitoring period, apply filters, control report modules included. options allow report tailored different monitoring projects, study designs, research aims.

title: Ecological Report
text: contents camReport object extracted inferred main camera-trap dataset. metadata can viewed modified using info() function. Use info(cm) see available metadata fields. Specific fields, report title, subtitle, authors, institute, site name, logo path, can also retrieved individually. needed, fields can updated generating final report using following steps:
code: # View all available metadata fields info(cm)  # Retrieve specific metadata fields info(cm, name = "title") info(cm, name = "subtitle") info(cm, name = "authors") info(cm, name = "institute") info(cm, name = "siteName") info(cm, name = "logoPath")  # Update metadata fields info(cm, name = "title") <- "Camera-Trap Monitoring Report: Hoge Veluwe National Park, the Netherlands" info(cm, name = "subtitle") <- "Ecological insights from long-term camera-trap data" info(cm, name = "authors") <- c("Elham Ebrahimi", "Patrick Jansen") info(cm, name = "institute") <- "Utrecht University, the Netherlands" info(cm, name = "siteName") <- "Hoge Veluwe National Park"  # Optional: add an institute or project logo info(cm, name = "logoPath") <- "path_to_logo/UU_logo.png"  # Check the updated metadata info(cm)

title: Ecological Report
text: Ecological Report can generated selected group species, referred focus_group. allows users focus report particular taxonomic ecological group, large_mammals, wild_mammals, wild_animals, birds, amphibians, domestic, custom-defined group. Users can also define new group assigning records based one fields, including scientificName, class, order, observationType. custom focus group can also created users want focus specific set species records. defining new group, useful check species table see species available dataset.
code: # Check the current focus group cm$setting$focus_groups  # View available group definitions names(cm$group_definition)  # Change the focus group cm$set_focus_group(x = "birds")  # Re-run setup after changing the focus group cm$setup()  # Inspect the rules used to define existing groups cm$get_group("large_mammals") cm$get_group("wild_mammals") cm$get_group("wild_animals") cm$get_group("birds") cm$get_group("amphibians") # Check the list of species available in the dataset cm$data_status$Species$Table  # Add a new custom group cm$add_group(   name = "target_species",   x = list(     scientificName = c(       "Vulpes vulpes",       "Capreolus capreolus",       "Sus scrofa"     )   ) )  # Use the new group as the focus group cm$set_focus_group(x = "target_species")  # Re-run setup after changing group definitions and focus group cm$setup()  # Check the updated focus group cm$setting$focus_groups

title: Ecological Report
text: Reports can generated available years dataset selected monitoring period. useful users want focus specific time window, single monitoring year multi-year period. count filter can also adjusted control minimum number observations required species included report.
code: # Check which years are available in the dataset cm$extractYears()  # Select a specific monitoring period cm$years <- 2023:2024  # Re-run setup after changing the selected years cm$setup()  # Check the current count filter cm$filterCount  # Change the minimum number of observations required cm$filterCount <- 10  # Generate the report using the selected years and filter settings report(cm, view = TRUE)

title: Ecological Report
text: Users can also control sections included final report. useful shorter report needed, analyses relevant particular dataset. Together, options allow users generate Ecological Reports reproducible, flexible, tailored aims camera-trap monitoring project.
code: # View the sections currently available listReportSections(cm)  # Show all section names section_names()  # Exclude one section section_names(exclude = "introduction")  # Exclude multiple sections section_names(exclude = c("acknowledgements", "appendix"))  # Keep only selected sections section_names(   keep = c("introduction", "methods", "study_area") )  # Example: include all sections except richness and co-occurrence selected_sections <- section_names(   exclude = c("richness", "co_occurrence") )  # Check the selected section names selected_sections  # Apply selected sections to the camReport object sections(cm, selected_sections)  # Generate the customised report report(cm, view = TRUE)  # Restore all available sections sections(cm, n = section_names())

title: Module Management
text: Ecological Report built separate report sections, referred modules. module small report component can contain explanatory text, R code, . modular structure allows users extend report adding new sections, modifying existing ones, changing order sections appear. new module defined YAML file registered camtrapReport using add_Module(). module added, useful test generating full report. modular structure camtrapReport allows users extend Ecological Report defining new report sections, registering modules, selecting modules included, generating final report. Figure 1. Modular workflow defining, registering, selecting, running report modules camtrapReport. Figure 1 summarises new modules added report workflow. module can defined using R code YAML file, registered module library, added main camReport object, tested separately, included generating final Ecological Report.

title: Module Management
text: adding new module, check modules already available package.
code: # List all available modules list_Modules()  # Show all section names used in the report section_names()

what: 2. Create a new module YAML file
title: Module Management
text: module can written YAML file. example defines simple module called species_table, adds species summary table Ecological Report. Create file called species_table.yml save working directory. name field defines internal module name. title field controls section title shown report. parent field defines module belongs report structure, example methods results. text field adds explanatory text, code field contains R code used generate module output.
code: name: species_table title: "Species summary table" parent: "results" text: "This section provides a summary of the species detected in the camera-trap dataset, including their taxonomy and total number of captures."  code: |   #| echo: false   #| message: false   #| warning: false      species_table <- object$data_status$Species$Table      if (!is.null(species_table) && nrow(species_table) > 0) {     knitr::kable(       species_table,       caption = "Species detected in the camera-trap dataset."     )   } else {     cat("No species table is available for this dataset.")   }

title: Module Management
text: creating YAML file, add module using add_Module(). example, new species_table module placed richness section. can change = "richness" another section name want new module appear elsewhere report.
code: # Add a new module from a YAML file add_Module(   x = "species_table.yml",   before = "richness",   test = FALSE,   object = cm )  # Check whether the module has been added list_Modules() section_names()

title: Module Management
text: camReport object already loaded adding module, run setup() update report sections.
code: # Re-run setup after adding the new module cm$setup()  # Get all available section names, including the new module n <- section_names()  # Apply the updated section list to the camReport object sections(cm, n)  # Check included sections listReportSections(cm)

title: Module Management
text: generating full report, test new module. useful checking whether YAML structure, text, R code work correctly. module opens correctly browser, can included full report.
code: # Test the new module only testSection(   cm$reportObjectElements$Modules$species_table,   object = cm,   view = TRUE )

title: Module Management
text: testing, generate full Ecological Report new module included. workflow allows users extend Ecological Report project-specific outputs, additional summaries, new figures, custom interpretation sections.
code: # Generate the full report report(cm, view = TRUE)

title: Package Overview
text: camtrapReport modular R package turning camera-trap data structured, reproducible ecological reports. package brings together data preparation, data-quality diagnostics, ecological analysis, visualisation, report generation single workflow. workflow built around central camReport object, created camData(). object stores input data, metadata, user settings, intermediate outputs, report sections, analytical modules. object, users can generate Data Status Check report using status() Ecological Report using report(). Figure 1. Overview camtrapReport workflow, input camera-trap data central camReport object final report outputs. Figure 1 summarises main camtrapReport workflow. Camera-trap data imported camData() stored central camReport object. object supports data-status checks, ecological analyses, visualisation, report customisation, automated report generation.

what: What camtrapReport is designed to do
title: Package Overview
text: camtrapReport designed bridge gap standardised camera-trap data reproducible ecological reporting. helps users assess whether dataset complete internally consistent, supports generation ecological outputs automated reproducible workflow. package intended support researchers, monitoring practitioners, conservation managers, wider camera-trap community reducing manual reporting effort improving consistency across projects, sites, survey periods, species groups.

title: Package Overview
text: workflow requires camera-trap dataset Camtrap-DP format, provided ZIP archive. Optional supporting data, habitat information study-area boundaries, can added improve visualisation, ecological analysis, interpretation.

title: Package Overview
text: Data Status Check report evaluates whether camera-trap dataset complete, internally consistent, suitable ecological analysis report generation. summarises data-quality dimensions completeness, consistency, annotation quality, validation status, temporal mismatches, spatial outliers, observation types.

title: Package Overview
text: Ecological Report structured, article-style document assembled selected analytical modules. combines ecological summaries, tables, figures, maps, narrative text reproducible report.

title: Package Overview
text: camtrapReport built modular system. report section generated one modules. module may contain text, R code, tables, figures, maps, ecological summaries. structure allows users select, remove, reorder, modify, add report modules depending aims project. New modules can also added using YAML templates custom R code, allowing users extend report workflow without changing package core.

title: Package Overview
text: camtrapReport provides unified workflow moving camera-trap data reproducible ecological outputs. combining data preparation, quality assessment, ecological analysis, visualisation, report generation modular framework, package helps users produce transparent consistent reports camera-trap datasets. two main outputs Data Status Check report Ecological Report. Together, help users assess data readiness, identify potential issues, translate camera-trap records interpretable ecological information. learn , continue Data Status Report, Ecological Report, add new modules? articles.

title: camtrapReport
text: camtrapReport helps turn camera-trap data standardised, reproducible ecological outputs workflow designed automate much reporting process possible. package combines data quality checks, summarisation, visualisation, ecological analysis modular framework, helping users move efficiently raw records interpretable results. automated workflow improves comparability, scalability, reproducibility across sites time, reducing technical barriers supporting transparent, efficient, timely ecological reporting. main functions package include: Data status report, performs automated diagnostics data quality completeness across key components camera-trap datasets, including spatial temporal information, key column availability, annotations, validation status, observation types capture method. checks help users identify missing information, inconsistencies, unusual values may require correction completion. Ecological report, produces standardised outputs modular components. Reports can include descriptive information, summaries key data, visualisations important patterns, ecological assessments analyses.

title: camtrapReport
text: camtrapReport designed datasets provided , converted , Camtrap-DP standard format, community-developed data exchange format camera-trap data. Several camera-trap data management systems support Camtrap-DP export format, including Agouti TRAPPER. also possible convert data camera-trap sources, including manually managed datasets Wildlife Insights exports, Camtrap-DP format use camtrapReport.

title: camtrapReport
text: Three example datasets provided . open-access camera-trap datasets, available GBIF, follow Camtrap-DP standard can used test camtrapReport. Note: habitat data study area files prepared exclusively testing camtrapReport represent official data products published original dataset owners.

title: camtrapReport
text: Install camtrapReport standard remotes workflow: Note: camtrapReport requires R ≥ 4.1.0. unsure current R version, run R.version.string R console.
code: # install using the "remotes" package if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_github("spatialecology/camtrapReport")

title: camtrapReport
text: addition loading camtrapReport package, depending report sections methods use, additional packages may required. ensure full functionality, run helper function, install_All, installs package dependencies: Note: may prompted approve package installations compile packages source. Make sure active internet connection.
code: library(camtrapReport) install_All()

title: camtrapReport
text: remotes::install_github() fails, common cause missing GitHub personal access token (PAT). set one , run: installation still fails, please open issue include full error message, output sessionInfo(), information whether error occurred install_github() install_All().
code: install.packages(c("remotes", "usethis", "gitcreds")) usethis::create_github_token()  # Opens GitHub in your browser — create and copy the token gitcreds::gitcreds_set()        # Paste the token when prompted in R remotes::install_github("spatialecology/camtrapReport")

title: camtrapReport
text: core camtrapReport mutable Reference Class object called camReport, acts central workflow container module registry. stores harmonised input data, user-defined settings, intermediate results throughout reporting process. Use camData() create camReport object reading pre-processing camera-trap dataset.

title: Install packages required by camtrapReport — install_All
text: pkgs Optional character vector package names install. `NULL`, default camtrapReport package list used. update Logical. `TRUE`, reinstall GitHub/GitLab packages attempt install update CRAN packages. include_dev Logical. `TRUE`, also install developer packages `testthat`, `pkgdown`, `covr`, `usethis` `devtools`. include_github Logical. `TRUE`, install required GitHub packages. include_gitlab Logical. `TRUE`, install required GitLab packages GitLab package list available. ... Additional arguments passed `install.packages()`.

title: Managing modules — add_Module
text: x new module YAML filename name module new module added name module new module added test logical; specifying whether module tested name module name parent parent name module (modules organised hierarchically parent childs) level0 Optional; names modules root: c("introduction", "methods", "results", "acknowledgement", "appendix") recursive logical; TRUE, deleting parent module, child modules also deleted avoid inconsistencies report id module id tree logical; specifies whether list modules represents hierarchical tree structure brief logical; tree FALSE, specifies whether brief module information returned include_trash logical; tree FALSE, specifies whether deleted modules trash also listed validate logical; whether modules tested validity

text: name character used unique name object. title character specifies title header subsection, appeared report parent NULL, subsection considered level 1, subsection can added second third level previously added subsections (.e., subsection subsection) txt character list texts. code_setting chunk R code provided code argument, setting chunk can provided within brackt; example:  { c(echo=FALSE,results="asis")  } packages character vector specifying name required packages code chunk R code can provided R script within bracket {   }

title: Creates a reproducible subsection of the report. — reportSection
text: name subsections unique, meaning two subsections can name. new subsection name previously added subsections added report, old one replaced new one. code chuck, get access camReport object fields methods, need use "object" code (see examples).

title: Creates a reproducible subsection of the report. — reportSection
text: Ebrahimi et al. (2025) xxx

title: Creates a reproducible subsection of the report. — reportSection
text: Elham Ebrahimi

title: Creates a reproducible subsection of the report. — reportSection
code: tx <- reportSection(name='introduction',title='Introduction',parent=NULL,txt="This is introduction section...")  tx #> class                                 : .textSection  #> ===========================================================  #> name of the object                    :  introduction  #> title                                 :  Introduction  #> parent                                :   #> is R code included?                   :  FALSE  #> -----------------------------------------------------------       # in the following text section, we add an R chunk code which gets access to the camReport object # through using the object keyword:  tx2 <- reportSection(name='method',title='Methods',parent=NULL,txt="Here, we show the usage of ...",                       code_setting={c(echo=FALSE,results="asis",warn=FALSE)},                       packages=c("gt","dplyr"),                       code = {                                                  object$camera_setup |>                         gt() |>                         tab_header(                         title = md("**Camera Deployment Summary**"),                         subtitle = md("**Table 1.** Details of camera deployments per year")) |>                         cols_label(                         year = "Year",                         number_camtraps = "Camera Traps",                         deployment_period = "Deployment Period",                         setup_period = "Setup Period",                         pickup_period = "Pickup Period") |>                         tab_options(                         table.font.size = px(14),                         heading.title.font.size = px(16),                         heading.subtitle.font.size = px(12),                         row.striping.include_table_body = TRUE) |>                         opt_row_striping() |>                         tab_style(style = list(                         cell_borders(sides = "bottom", color = "gray", weight = px(1))),                         locations = cells_body()) |>                         tab_style(                         style = list(cell_text(weight = "bold")),locations = cells_column_labels())                                                                         }                       )                        tx2 #> class                                 : .textSection  #> ===========================================================  #> name of the object                    :  method  #> title                                 :  Methods  #> parent                                :   #> is R code included?                   :  TRUE  #> -----------------------------------------------------------

title: Updating content of report sections — updateReportSection
text: x camReport object created camData function section character refer report section (name title report) text optional, update text body report section title optional, new title section code optional, update add R code chunk code_name optional, R code chunk provided, name can specified code_setting optional, setting new R chunk packages optional, name R packages required new R chunk append_text optional; whether new text appended previous text (default: FALSE) append_code optional; whether new R code appended previous R code (default: FALSE)

title: Update a report section — updateReportSection
text: Updates title, text, code chunk, code settings package list existing report section `camReport` object.

title: Update a report section — updateReportSection
code: updateReportSection(   x,   section,   text,   title,   code,   code_name,   code_setting,   packages,   append_text,   append_code )  # S4 method for class 'camReport' updateReportSection(   x,   section,   text,   title,   code,   code_name,   code_setting,   packages,   append_text,   append_code )

title: Update a report section — updateReportSection
text: x `camReport` object. section single character string identifying section name title. text Optional replacement text. title Optional replacement title. code Optional replacement appended R code. Code can supplied character string inside braces. code_name Optional code chunk name. Required section contains multiple chunks specific chunk updated. code_setting Optional R Markdown chunk settings. packages Optional character vector packages required chunk. append_text Logical. `TRUE`, append text existing section text. append_code Logical. `TRUE`, append code existing code chunk.

title: Update a report section — updateReportSection
text: Invisibly returns updated `camReport` object.

title: Resources
text: camtrapReport designed camera-trap community. Organisations, research teams, camera-trap networks interested arranging workshops, exploring collaboration opportunities, contributing new camtrapReport modules welcome get touch email e.ebrahimi@uu.nl via LinkedIn.

title: Getting help with camtrapReport
text: Use GitHub issue tracker : reproducible bugs broken outputs installation problems include error message Please include: camtrapReport version R version operating system minimal reproducible example sessionInfo() full error message usage questions, workflow advice, interpretation questions, please use Discussions package documentation instead opening bug report.

title: Tutorials
text: page presents example outputs camtrapReport, including results Data Status Check Ecological Report Generation.
